zh: 地球历2015年五月九日20点
姜海已经回到了他的出租屋里，
姜海的出租屋位于振华长三角电子厂附近的城中村，是一栋老旧红砖楼的顶层单间。
楼下巷道狭窄拥挤，终日弥漫着油烟和潮湿的霉味，窗外的电线如蛛网般交错，远处可见工厂烟囱的轮廓。
房间不足20平方米，进门即见床铺，墙角挤着一张掉漆的木桌和塑料凳。
唯一的窗户正对隔壁楼的墙壁，采光极差，白天也需开灯。
地面铺着碎裂的瓷砖，墙皮因潮湿大片剥落，露出灰黄的水泥。
门后挂钩挂着深蓝色工装，沾满机油的袖口已硬化成块。
另一侧墙面上挂着一个老式挂表，滴滴哒哒的转动着，一看就是好久没擦过了，框体上沿积攒着厚实的灰尘。
床头堆着车工钳工教材和翻烂的《机械原理手册》，桌角立着半瓶廉价二锅头。
"这货还会喝酒？"
姜海感到一股既陌生又熟悉的感觉侵袭着大脑。毕竟，脑子里被塞入一些不是自己的记忆并不是什么好事。
"还是洗个手先"
姜海在以前蓝星的时候是个爱干净的人，回家第一件事情就是去洗手，这个习惯改不了。
来到卫生间，看着镜子，姜海终于看到了现在的自己。
身高约1米75，因长期伏案编程和近期车间劳作，肩背微驼但肌肉紧实。
瘦削的脸颊凹陷，眼下两片青黑，像是被生活反复捶打后尚未消退的淤痕。
"剑眉星目！"
姜海仔细打量着自己，觉得论长相还算过得去，应该适合电视剧的男主角。
"哪怕不是男主，演一个重要反派也行哈"，姜海自嘲着。
【您好宿主，你在平民世界领域的首次任务即将开启……】
【请注意系统任务提示和说明注意事项】
此时姜海的系统提示音已经响起。
"这就要开始了？有点小激动哈"
这是姜海首个任务，不由得他打起十二分精神。
咔咔咔
只听见周围出现奇怪的咔咔声响，仿佛在不知哪里打开了一扇巨大的铁门。
这种巨响让第一次听见的人不由得汗毛倒竖、毛骨悚然。
姜海周围的空气骤然凝固，老式的挂表指针已经不再转动，也听不见滴答声。
"时空凝固了？"
正当姜海纳闷和检视周围环境时，系统提示音响起：
【宿主，目前进入传送阶段，您的周围10米范围内的时空暂停，方便您进入传送口】
【此阶段不会受任何外力干扰，放心进入。】
"原来如此，这估计就是安排任务的一种保护性措施，防止意外发生吧"。
姜海曾经看过一篇报道，讲一个能穿越墙体的特异功能者，一次意外嵌入了一堵墙里，当场就死了。
估计就是做这种类似穿越的时候没有任何保护措施导致吧。
正思索着，突然，姜海面前出现一个光圈，看着像是一个传送门。
【请宿主进入传送门】
听着提示指示，姜海毫不犹豫，向传送门走去。
踏出一步，如踏出万里。姜海眼前看到景物瞬间变换，如梦如画，绚丽多彩，不可用言语描绘。
只一瞬，姜海发现进入到了一个非常巨大的大厅之中，还有很多人，像是进入到了一个集市中。
大厅的周围有着四通八达的通道，每个通道上都有指示牌子，距离太远，暂时看不清上面写的字。
姜海回头看了一眼身后，居然竖立着一块石碑。上面刻着看不懂的符号，也不知道是什么意思，此时石碑上隐隐的光晕在褪去。
"这应该就是传送门吧，估计回去的时候走这里？但这个石碑怎么激发，是摸一下吗？"
正当姜海心里估计着是不是摸一下石碑看看有没有什么反应的时候，
系统提示音响起：
【宿主，请根据观察指示牌，前去1001号通道，到23号门领取任务】
"这个接任务的方式还不错，给个赞"
姜海对于这种接任务的形式表示满意，他看过一些穿越小说，都是无厘头的突然给个任务，然后给三个选择，三种奖励云云，搞得是像玩游戏那样突兀和生硬。太不敬业了，哪像现在这个系统，这个服务，肯定要给五星好评哈。
提示音又响起：
【宿主的五星好评已经收到，本系统会再接再厉，继续给您带来优质的服务！】
"居然能自动听见自己的心声？这个系统简直神了"
姜海诧异了一下。
"1001号通道，怎么找？"
姜海自言自语道。
这个大厅实在是巨大，远处的牌子都看不清，有点难办。
这时，刚经过的一个路人听见了姜海的自言自语，转头说到：
"哥们新来的？看一下附近的通道牌子，然后根据牌子就能找到1001了，这边是1层，所以都是10开头"
这人应该是有事，迅速的说完就走了，连姜海的谢谢的声音估计也没听见。
"这年头好人还是多"
姜海赞叹一下，然后迅速走向附近的一个通道口，
这标识也很明显，牌子上大黑体字是1023，左右有个指示 向左方是1022，向右方1024。
"明白了！，我要去的1001很容易找到嘛"
姜海朝着1022方向走去 ，经过下一个路口果然看见1022，那推测没错继续找就能找到1001。
一路无话。
姜海顺利的找到1001通道口，往里看去，左右都是一扇扇的门，然后顺着通道口往里走，找到了23号门。
23号门上挂着一个袋子，不知道里面是什么，姜海没有贸然打开。而是礼貌的敲了敲门。
不一会儿，23号门打开了，里面粗看是个办公室，有好几张办公桌，都坐着人，有男有女，叽叽喳喳的聊着天，有些人磕着瓜子，咔吧咔吧的，有些人喝着茶，看着报纸。
开门的是一个中年男人，戴着一副深框眼镜。
"是姜海吧？请从门口袋子里摸一个号，然后随我来"。
深框眼镜男确认了姜海身份后，直接让姜海摸一个号。
姜海伸手到门口挂着的袋里摸了一下，感觉袋子里是一个个玻璃球，没多想，随手掏了一个。
拿出一看，果然是个玻璃球，里面有字，太小，看不清。
然后跟着深框眼镜男，走到了他的办公桌旁。
"现在请把抽到的号放在桌上放大器里"
姜海这才发现办公桌上有个大盘子，架着一个放大镜，玻璃球放在盘子里，通过放大镜就能清晰的看见里面的内容。
"嗯，新手岛"
深框眼镜男抬了抬眼镜，皱眉道。
"新手岛怎么了？我是新人，新手岛任务不是很正常吗？"
姜海有点纳闷。
"呵呵，不是这个意思，一般来说第一次接任务的人是去新手村或新手区"
"新手岛任务嘛……"
深框眼镜男神秘的一笑。
"别紧张，去了你就知道了，虽然不知道你第一任务是去干什么，但应该不难"
"好吧，谢谢，不知道大哥怎么称呼？"
姜海作为上一世的码农，这点人情世故还是懂的，嘴上要殷勤，见男的喊哥，见女的喊美女。不管美丑，没多大问题。任何一个时空都管用。
深框眼镜男果然笑了笑，指着办公桌上的指示牌介绍到，我是这里的管理员005号，你就叫我005号就行。这里看来都是使用编号来称呼，匿名的。
"好的005号大哥，"，虽然姜海喊起来感觉怪怪的，总感觉是在喊什么捏脚技师那样。
"那接下来我怎么去新手岛？"
"待会儿你就知道了"
005号神秘一笑。
说罢,005号管理员将写着新手岛的玻璃球交到姜海手上。
此时姜海脑海的系统提示音再次响起：
【地标识别体已记录，宿主准备传送至新手岛，倒计时开始】
姜海只觉眼前一花，整个人好像被什么吸进去一样，消失在办公室里。
周围的人也见怪不怪，只是随意的朝005号管理员这边看了一眼，就各忙各的去了。
005号管理员坐回了他的座位上，悠闲的对着傍边的邻桌人说到：
"下个敲门的是你去开喽……"
en: May 9, 2015, Earth Calendar. 8:00 PM.
Jiang Hai was back in his rental unit.
His room sat at the top of an old red-brick walk-up in the urban village wedged behind the Zhenhua Yangtze River Delta Electronics Factory.

The alley below was cramped and perpetually hazy, thick with the stench of cooking oil and damp rot. Outside his window, power lines crisscrossed like a spider's fever dream. In the distance, factory smokestacks rose against the night sky.
The room itself was under 200 square feet. Open the door and you were already at the bed. A chipped wooden desk and a plastic stool fought for space in the corner.
The lone window faced straight into the brick wall of the neighboring building. Light was a luxury—he had to keep the lights on even at noon.
The floor was cracked tile. The walls had peeling patches the size of dinner plates, exposing gray-yellow cement beneath.
On the hook behind the door hung a set of dark-blue work overalls. The cuffs were caked with machine oil, hardened into stiff crusts.
On the opposite wall, an old wind-up clock ticked away—thock, thock, thock—its frame coated in a thick ridge of dust that clearly hadn't been touched in forever.
Beside the bed: a stack of lathe and fitter textbooks, a battered Manual of Mechanical Principles, and on the corner of the desk, half a bottle of cheap erguotou liquor.
*This guy actually drank?*
A wave of something alien yet eerily familiar washed over Jiang Hai's brain. Having someone else's memories forcibly crammed into your skull? Not exactly a pleasant experience.
*First things first—wash my hands.*
Back on Blue Star, Jiang Hai had been a clean freak. Coming home meant washing hands, no exceptions. Some habits you just can't shake.
He stepped into the bathroom and finally—finally—got a look at his new face in the mirror.
About 175 centimeters. Shoulders slightly hunched from years of coding slouch, but the recent factory work had hardened the muscle underneath. Lean.
Gaunt cheeks, hollowed out. Two dark circles under his eyes, bruises that life had punched into him and never let fade.
*Sword brows, star eyes!*
Jiang Hai scrutinized his reflection from every angle. Honestly? Not bad. He had the face of a TV drama lead.
*Even if I'm not the hero, I could at least land a solid villain role,* he chuckled to himself.
[Ding]
[Host, your first mission in the Common People's World Realm is about to commence...]
[Please pay close attention to all system mission prompts and instructions.]
There it was. The system chime had rung.
*It's starting already? Not gonna lie—I'm a little hyped.*
His very first mission. Jiang Hai snapped to full alert, every nerve dialed to eleven.
*Klang—klang—KRRK.*
Weird metallic grinding sounds filled the air, like some colossal iron door being dragged open in a place that shouldn't exist.
The sound alone was enough to make a first-timer's hair stand on end. Pure nightmare fuel.
The air around Jiang Hai congealed. The old clock on the wall—frozen. The ticking? Dead silent.
*Did time just... stop?*
As Jiang Hai stood there baffled, scanning his surroundings, the system chimed in:
[Host: Transmission phase initiated. Spacetime within a 10-meter radius has been paused to facilitate your entry into the portal.]
[This phase is immune to all external interference. You may proceed without concern.]
*Ah, so that's it. Must be a safeguard built into the mission deployment—prevents accidents.*
Jiang Hai remembered reading a report once about a psychic who could phase through walls. One slip—one mistake—and he materialized halfway inside a concrete wall. Died on the spot.
This kind of teleportation without protection? Instant death.
As the thought passed, a ring of light bloomed in front of him. A portal.
[Ding]
[Host, please enter the portal.]
Jiang Hai didn't hesitate. He stepped forward.
One step—and it felt like crossing a thousand miles. The world around him erupted into colors he had no words for, a cascade of dreamscapes and painted light that defied description.
In an instant, he found himself standing inside a cavernous atrium. Crowds of people milled about—it felt like stepping into some interdimensional marketplace.
All around the perimeter, corridors branched off in every direction. Each one had a sign. They were too far away to read just yet.
Jiang Hai glanced over his shoulder. Behind him stood a stone tablet, etched with symbols he couldn't decipher. A faint glow was receding from its surface.
*So that's the portal gate, huh? Probably how I get back. But how do you activate this thing—just touch it?*
Before he could test the theory, the system chimed again:
[Host: please locate Corridor 1001 following the directional signs. Proceed to Door 23 to accept your mission.]
*Not a bad way to pick up quests. I'll give it a like.*

Jiang Hai was genuinely impressed by the system's UX. He'd read enough transmigration slop to know the drill—some random ding out of nowhere, three dialogue options, three reward tiers, all served up with the clunky artificiality of a bad RPG. Zero effort. Zero immersion. But this system? The service? Five stars. Easy.
[Ding]
Another chime:
[Host: Your five-star rating has been received! This system will continue to strive for service excellence!]
*It can hear my inner thoughts? This system is absurdly broken.*
Jiang Hai blinked in genuine disbelief.
*Corridor 1001... how do I find it?*
He muttered it aloud without thinking. The atrium was monstrous—the signs in the distance were illegible.
Just then, a passerby overheard him and glanced over:
"New guy? Check the nearest corridor sign. Follow the numbers and you'll find 1001. This is Level 1, so all corridors start with 10."
The guy seemed to be in a hurry—he rattled it off and vanished into the crowd before Jiang Hai could even get a "thanks" out.
*Still good people in the world.*
Jiang Hai nodded to himself, then strode toward the nearest corridor entrance.
The signage couldn't have been clearer. Bold block numbers: 1023. Arrows pointing left to 1022, right to 1024.
*Got it! 1001 should be easy.*
He headed left toward 1022. At the next junction—yep, 1022. Logic held. Keep going and he'd hit 1001.
He found it without incident. Corridor 1001 stretched before him, lined on both sides with numbered doors. He walked down until he reached Door 23.
A pouch hung from the door handle. No idea what was inside. Jiang Hai didn't touch it—instead, he knocked politely.

A moment later, the door swung open. Inside was something between a bullpen and a break room—several desks crammed with people, men and women alike, chattering away. Some cracking sunflower seeds between their teeth with rhythmic snaps. Others sipping tea, flipping through newspapers.
The man who answered the door was middle-aged, wearing thick-rimmed glasses.
"Jiang Hai, right? Reach into the pouch on the door and draw a number. Then follow me."
The man with the glasses confirmed Jiang Hai's identity and went straight to business.
Jiang Hai reached into the pouch. His fingers brushed against what felt like glass marbles. Without overthinking it, he grabbed one.
He pulled it out—a marble. Definitely a marble. Something was written inside it, but the text was way too small to read.
He followed Glasses to a desk.
"Now place your drawn number onto the magnifier."
That's when Jiang Hai noticed it: a large dish on the desk with a magnifying lens mounted above it. Place the marble in the dish, look through the lens, and the text inside became crystal clear.
"Hmph. Novice Island."
Glasses adjusted his frames and frowned.
"What's wrong with Novice Island? I'm new here—isn't an island mission normal for a rookie?"
Jiang Hai was puzzled.
"Heh. That's not what I meant. Usually, first-timers draw Novice Village. Or Novice Zone."
"Novice Island, on the other hand..."
A cryptic smile crept across Glasses' face.
"Don't sweat it. You'll find out when you get there. I don't know what your first mission entails, but it shouldn't be too rough."
"Alright, thanks. What should I call you, brother?"
Jiang Hai had spent a lifetime as a coder—he knew how to work people. Call every guy "brother," every woman "beauty." Ugly or gorgeous, didn't matter. Universal law. Worked in any dimension.
Glasses cracked a smile, just as predicted. He pointed to the nameplate on his desk. "I'm Admin 005. Just call me 005. Everyone here goes by numbers. Anonymous."
"Got it, Brother 005." Even as the words left his mouth, Jiang Hai cringed internally—it sounded way too much like he was addressing his massage therapist.
"So... how do I actually get to Novice Island?"
"You'll know soon enough."
That same cryptic grin.
005 handed the Novice Island marble to Jiang Hai.
[Ding]
The system chime filled Jiang Hai's mind once more:
[Landmark signature registered. Preparing teleportation to Novice Island. Countdown initiated.]
Jiang Hai's vision swam. A force yanked him—like being sucked through a straw—and he vanished from the office.
No one batted an eye. A couple of heads turned lazily toward Admin 005, then went right back to whatever they were doing.
Admin 005 sank back into his chair, leaned over to the person at the next desk, and said with a yawn:
"Next knock... you're up."
